commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a shared `sanitizeUrl()` helper and applies it to data-driven `href` attributes on the homepage, shoutouts, showcase, press, and localized trust pages.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main maps JSON and i18n URL fields directly into anchor `href` values, and the PR body adds terminal red-green build output for the malicious testimonial URL path. I did not run a local build during this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can resolve the current merge conflicts by preserving both current avatar-cache helpers and the sanitizer changes; no product decision is needed.

target.title: fix: validate URL protocols in data-driven href attributes to prevent XSS
target.body: ## Problem URL fields from JSON data files (`testimonials.json`, `testimonials-extra.json`, `community-builds.json`, `press.json`) are rendered directly in `` attributes. Astro auto-escapes HTML entities but does not block dangerous protocols like `javascript:`, `data:`, or `vbscript:`. If a crafted URL like `javascript:alert(document.cookie)` were merged via a community PR, it would render as a clickable XSS link on the homepage, shoutouts, showcase, and press pages. ## Fix Add a `sanitizeUrl()` utility in `src/lib/sanitize-url.ts` that validates URL protocols at build time, allowing only `http:`, `https:`, and `mailto:`. Dangerous protocols are replaced with `#`. All existing URLs are `https://` so there is no behavioral change. The function also handles percent-encoded bypass attempts (e.g., `%6a%61%76%61%73%63%72%69%70%74` for `javascript`) by decoding with `decodeURIComponent` before parsing, and strips ASCII control characters that browsers silently remove. **What changed:** - **New file** `src/lib/sanitize-url.ts`: utility that checks URL protocol against an allowlist - **7 pages updated** to wrap data-driven `href` attributes with `sanitizeUrl()`: - `index.astro` (testimonials, community builds, press) - `shoutouts.astro` (testimonials) - `showcase.astro` (featured builds) - `press.astro` (press articles) - `trust/ja/index.astro`, `trust/ko/index.astro`, `trust/zh-cn/index.astro` (i18n trust pages) **Protocols blocked:** | Protocol | Example | Blocked? | |----------|---------|----------| | `https:` | `https://x.com/...` | Allowed | | `http:` | `http://example.com` | Allowed | | `mailto:` | `mailto:a@b.com` | Allowed | | `javascript:` | `javascript:alert(1)` | Blocked | | `data:` | `data:text/html,...` | Blocked | | `vbscript:` | `vbscript:MsgBox(1)` | Blocked | Follows up on #140 and #142 which fixed related XSS vectors in the threat model overlay and avatar fallbacks. ## Real behavior proof Behavior addressed: Data-driven `href` attributes in 7 Astro pages rendered dangerous protocols (`javascript:`, `data:`) without sanitization. A crafted URL in any JSON data file would create a clickable XSS link in the built HTML. Real environment tested: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2), Node.js v22.16.0, Astro site built from source at `/tmp/fix-143`. Exact steps or command run after this patch: Step 1 -- inject a malicious `javascript:alert(document.cookie)` URL into `src/data/testimonials.json`. Step 2 -- build the site with `npx astro build`. Step 3 -- grep the built HTML for the injected entry. Step 4 -- revert the fix (checkout pre-fix pages from origin/main) and rebuild. Step 5 -- grep the built HTML again to show the dangerous URL renders raw. Evidence after fix: terminal output copied below. **After fix (sanitized):** ```console $ grep -oP 'href="[^"]*"[^>]*class="testimonial-card"[^>]*>.*?xss-proof' dist/index.html href="#" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="testimonial-card" tabindex="-1" data-astro-cid-j7pv25f6> ]*class="testimonial-card"[^>]*>.*?xss-proof' dist/index.html href="javascript:alert(document.cookie)" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="testimonial-card" tabindex="-1" data-astro-cid-j7pv25f6>
target.author: sebtardif

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: SebTardif
